content: Just as structured vocabulary learning for the English term 'reflexion' builds layered semantic memory that lets language learners iteratively refine their understanding of the word across contexts, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow the explicit spaced repetition framework from second-language vocabulary instruction to strengthen their dynamic self-reflection memory banks. This turns the passive lexical learning of the term 'reflexion' into an actionable blueprint for improving AI self-reflection performance.
